case_name: Pol v Accident Compensation Corporation (Entitlements) [2022] NZACC 88 (12 May 2022)

summary.brief: Pol v Accident Compensation Corporation (Entitlements) [2022] NZACC 88 (12 May 2022). Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: On 2 August 2017, Mr Pol visited his GP who lodged a claim for a lumbar sprain and left sided sciatica, said to have been caused by the accident on 28 May 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Corporation obtained advice from Branch Medical Advisor, Dr Shanali Jayawardhana. On 18 October 2017, he noted that Mr Pol was recorded to have been suffering from low back pain since June 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Shanali Jayawardhana

evidence_index.text: In early 2018, the Corporation arranged for Mr Pol to be assessed by Mr Bill Sanderson, Orthopaedic Surgeon, to help clarify the scope of injuries caused by the July 2017 accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Court finds that the Corporation reasonably required Mr Pol, as the recipient of weekly compensation, to undergo assessment by Mr Ferguson, a registered Orthopaedic Surgeon.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Appeal 217/21 against the decision of a Reviewer dated 31 August 2021 is dismissed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: On 6 November 2015, Mr Pol suffered a fall from a ladder. On 9 November 2015, he was granted cover for a left wrist injury.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 28 May 2017, Mr Pol suffered a further accident when he was bending over to shear a sheep. He was granted cover for a lumbar sprain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 13 July 2017, Mr Pol slipped on a deck and fell on his back. On 21 July 2017, cover was granted for contusions to his chest and back, and for a thoracic vertebrae fracture at the T10 level.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Sanderson advised that Mr Pol was suffering from changes to the discs in his lumbar spine which pre-dated the July 2017 accident and had merely been exacerbated by it.
evidence_index.speaker: Mr. Bill Sanderson

evidence_index.text: The Court finds, in light of Mr Pol’s unreasonable refusal or failure to comply with the Corporation’s reasonable requirement that he undergo assessment by Mr Ferguson, that the Corporation was entitled to suspend Mr Pol’s weekly compensation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Low back pain and left-sided sciatica
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mr Pol reports low back pain and left-sided sciatica following an accident on 28 May 2017.
past_medical_history: Left wrist injury from a fall on 6 November 2015
Lumbar sprain from an accident on 28 May 2017
Thoracic vertebrae fracture at T10 from a fall on 13 July 2017

focused_exam.spine: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: MRI Spine
requestables.description: MRI of the lumbar and thoracic spine

reveal.result_summary: Fracture of T10 vertebrae and annular tear at L5/S1

requestables.name: Orthopaedic Assessment
requestables.description: Assessment by Mr Bill Sanderson, Orthopaedic Surgeon

reveal.result_summary: Degenerative changes in lumbar spine exacerbated by accident

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with back pain and sciatica

key_events.event: GP visit and claim lodged for lumbar sprain

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with ongoing back pain and sciatica
decision_points.prompt: Should the patient undergo further assessment by a spinal specialist?

options.description: Refer to spinal specialist for further assessment
options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to clarify the scope of injuries and guide rehabilitation

options.description: Do not refer for further assessment
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient refused to attend without further response from CEO

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not ensure patient attended assessment
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient insisted on response from CEO before attending

expected_action_court.description: Referral to spinal specialist should have been pursued

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to ensure compliance with necessary medical assessment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral and assessment by spinal specialist
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Requirement to comply with rehabilitation plan

patient_outcome.description: Ongoing back pain with limited work capability
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic pain, unable to return to previous occupation

key_findings: Corporation's decision to suspend entitlements was upheld
Patient's refusal to attend assessment deemed unreasonable

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Patient's refusal to comply with assessment
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Assessment could have clarified injury scope

factual_timeline.event: GP visit and claim lodged for lumbar sprain

tests_performed.result: Fracture of T10 vertebrae and annular tear at L5/S1

allegations.allegation: Unreasonable refusal to attend medical assessment

court_findings.finding: Corporation's decision to suspend entitlements was upheld
court_findings.legal_significance: Patient's refusal to attend assessment deemed unreasonable
